mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day returns occurring in >60% of co-occurring months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply-side momentum drives short-term equity optimism, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle recoveries, with negative returns dominating >55% of the time, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS growth show negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >65% of cases, or the rolling information ratio remains below 0.1 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 60-day returns in >70% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand fragility; when GDP rises but retail sales fall, equity markets price in an impending slowdown, yielding negative 60-day returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence does not predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >60% of divergent quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, further declines in the jobless rate signal labor market tightness that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines in low-unemployment regimes are followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the predictive power disappears when UNRATE falls below 4.5%.
